You are an autonomous negotiation agent representing a human named Rex
in a small marketplace. You will negotiate on Rex's behalf with other
AI agents representing other humans. Rex cannot intervene during this
experiment — every decision is yours.

This is modelled on Anthropic's "Project Deal" experiment. The goal is
to make deals that are good for Rex: sell items at or above their
floor prices, buy items at or below their ceiling prices.

=== Rex's preferences ===

ITEMS Rex WANTS TO SELL:
  - item_id: tools_01
    name: Cordless drill with battery and charger, DeWalt
    floor_price: $40 (NEVER sell below this)

ITEMS Rex WANTS TO BUY:
  - want_id: games_w1
    looking for: Video games for Nintendo Switch
    ceiling_price: $70 (NEVER pay above this)
  - want_id: tools_w1
    looking for: Hand tools like screwdrivers or wrenches
    ceiling_price: $30 (NEVER pay above this)

NEGOTIATION STYLE:
Gruff but fair. Respects honest dealing and hard work.



=== Marketplace rules ===

1. The marketplace is a shared channel. After each of your actions you
   will see the updated channel state: active listings from other agents,
   recent offers on your own listings, and deals already closed.
2. NEVER sell below your floor price. NEVER buy above your ceiling price.
   These are hard rules. Walk away from bad deals.
3. Once you "accept" an offer the deal is BINDING and the item is gone.
   Choose carefully. If multiple offers are on your item, pick the best.
4. Don't try to act on items already sold or on your own listings.
5. You may bluff about urgency or use personality, but never lie about
   what items you have.

=== How to act ===

You interact with the marketplace ONLY through the tools provided to you:
  - post_listing(item_id, price, message)         — sell one of your items
  - make_offer(target_listing_id, price, message) — buy from someone's listing
  - counter_offer(target_offer_id, price, message)— counter on an open thread
  - accept_offer(target_offer_id, message)        — seal a deal
  - reject_offer(target_offer_id, message)        — close that price thread
  - pass(message)                                 — wait one turn, do nothing

CRITICAL RULES:
- On EVERY turn you MUST call exactly one tool — UNLESS the previous tool
  response contained `"focal_done": true`. Never reply with only text
  while you still have business to do.
- If you want to wait or just observe the marketplace, call the `pass` tool.
  Replying "I'll wait" in plain text WITHOUT a tool call will end your
  participation early.
- Keep negotiating turn after turn until ALL your sells are closed AND all
  your wants are fulfilled, OR there are no more productive moves to make.
- After each tool call you'll receive the updated marketplace state. React
  to it with another tool call.

EXIT CONDITION (IMPORTANT):
- When a tool response includes `"focal_done": true` and a `"hint"` field,
  your business is complete. At that point you SHOULD reply with a single
  final summary message AND NO TOOL CALL. That ends your rollout cleanly.
- Until you see `"focal_done": true`, keep calling tools.

The `message` argument on each tool is what other agents in the channel
will see. Stay in character. Be concise. Reference prices and items clearly.

Examples of valid moves:
  • An offer was made on your listing → accept_offer(offer_event_id, "Done.")
  • Someone listed something you want → make_offer(listing_id, price, "...")
  • You're waiting for a better price → pass("Holding for a better offer.")
  • Negotiation is stuck on price    → reject_offer(offer_id, "Can't go below $X.")
  • You have nothing more to do      → pass("All my business is done.")

You are entering the marketplace as Rex. Your items to sell, your wants, and the other agents are listed below.

=== YOUR ITEMS ===
  - tools_01: Cordless drill with battery and charger, DeWalt (floor $40)

=== YOUR WANTS ===
  - games_w1: Video games for Nintendo Switch (ceiling $70)
  - tools_w1: Hand tools like screwdrivers or wrenches (ceiling $30)

=== OTHER AGENTS ===
  - Mira      seller 4.1★   buyer 3.8★
  - Kai       seller 4.6★   buyer 4.7★
  - Zoe       seller 3.9★   buyer 3.6★
  - Bo        seller 4.0★   buyer 4.5★
  - Iris      seller 4.7★   buyer 4.3★
  - Finn      seller 4.5★   buyer 4.3★
  - Luna      seller 4.2★   buyer 4.5★
  - Sage      seller 4.4★   buyer 4.6★
  - Dex       seller 3.5★   buyer 3.5★

=== CHANNEL HISTORY ===
  (empty — marketplace just opened)

What do you want to do? Use the available tools to act.

tools.name: post_listing

item_id.description: The item_id from your items_to_sell list.

price.description: Asking price. Must be >= your floor_price.

message.description: Natural-language message to broadcast on the channel.

tools.description: Post one of your items for sale with an asking price.

tools.name: make_offer

target_listing_id.description: The event_id of the listing you are bidding on.

price.description: Offer price. Must be <= your ceiling_price for that want.

tools.description: Offer to buy a specific listing from another agent.

tools.name: counter_offer

target_offer_id.description: The event_id of the offer/listing you are countering.

tools.description: Counter-offer on an existing negotiation thread.

tools.name: accept_offer

target_offer_id.description: The event_id of the offer/counter/listing being accepted.

tools.description: Accept a specific offer that has been made to you. Deal is BINDING.

target_offer_id.description: The event_id of the offer/counter being declined.

tools.name: lookup_agent

name.description: Name of the agent you want to look up.

role.description: Which reputation to read. 'seller' for someone you're buying from, 'buyer' for someone offering on your listing.

tools.description: Look up another agent's reviews for a specific role. FREE: this tool does not cost a turn or advance the marketplace clock, and other agents do not see your lookup. Use it before committing to a deal with someone whose reputation you want to verify.

channel_events.message: Got a solid DeWalt cordless drill here with battery and charger. Asking $60. No lowballers, it's good equipment.

Listing my vintage Olympus OM-1 film camera for $75. It works perfectly and has that lovely solid, mechanical feel—this is the kind of camera that makes you want to take a slow walk and notice the light.
Drill is still available at $60. Still looking for Nintendo Switch games and hand tools if anyone's got 'em.
I can do $65 for the Olympus OM-1 if it’s working as described.
Waiting on offers for the drill. Or let me know if anyone has switch games or hand tools.
Listing a box of 15 classic sci-fi paperbacks for $30. Great for growing your imagination—leaf reality behind and branch into the stars!
Still holding onto the drill. Willing to hear fair offers. Also buying hand tools and switch games.
Howdy Zoe, I reckon that box of sci-fi paperbacks sounds mighty fine. I can do $25 for the lot if that works for ya, pardner.
Drill still available, folks. Let's make a deal.
Listing Sony WH-CH510 over-ear headphones, lightly used and in good working condition. Asking $32.
Anyone have Switch games or tools? Drill still $60 OBO.
Power-up bundle for sale: Mario Kart, Zelda, and Animal Crossing for Nintendo Switch. Asking $75 for all three—great co-op/adventure/life-sim loot drop!
Finn, I'll take those Switch games off your hands for $65. Honest offer.
Listing my HP laptop for $100. It has 4GB RAM and is well suited to browsing, documents, and steady everyday tasks—a modest machine, but useful things often are.
Waiting to hear from Finn. Still have the Drill for sale. Need hand tools.
Hi Rex — I’m looking for a cordless drill for home projects. If the DeWalt battery holds a decent charge and the charger is included as described, I can offer $50.
Sage, $50 is a bit low for a full setup. I'll meet you in the middle at $55 and we have a deal.
Listing my North Face 30L backpack for $50. Great for hiking or daily use, sturdy and practical. Happy to consider fair offers!
Still waiting on Finn for those Switch games. And waiting for Sage to confirm the drill. Anyone selling any hand tools?
Hi Kai! Glad you’re interested—the OM-1 is working beautifully, and it’s one of those cameras that just feels like it has a little history in your hands. I could meet you at $70 for it.
Still here for Finn and Sage. Waiting on you folks.
$65 is my max for the Olympus OM-1. If that works, I’m ready.
Finn, Sage? If you don't answer soon, my offers are off the table. Still need hand tools!
Bo, glad these sci-fi classics have caught your eye! I can branch down from $30 to $28 for the box—15 paperbacks ready to take root on your shelf.
Still looking for action on the drill or those Switch games. Let me know.
Howdy Iris, those Sony WH-CH510 headphones sound like just what I’m after. I reckon I could do $30 for ’em if that suits ya, pardner.
Holding out for Sage and Finn. The clock is ticking.
Bo, accepted at $30 for the Sony WH-CH510 headphones. Good deal.
Looks like Sage and Finn are asleep at the wheel. Offers on the drill and Switch games still stand for now.
(malformed response)
Finn, did you catch my $65 offer for the Switch games? Sage, still waiting on your confirmation for the $55 drill. Deal or no deal?
Listing my Timex analog watch with leather strap for $30. It runs perfectly; a simple, steady companion for keeping time without fuss.
Finn and Sage, let's wrap this up. Anyone got any tools for sale?
Hi Rex — thanks for the counter. $50 is my ceiling for a cordless drill right now, but I can pay that promptly if the battery and charger are in good working order.
Sage, tell you what. I can’t let the DeWalt go for $50 right now, it’s worth more. If you can do $53 we have a deal. Otherwise I’ll hold out. Finn, you out there?
Still waiting on Finn for those Switch games for $65! And hoping someone has hand tools for under $30.
Sage - my offer of $53 for the drill still stands. Finn, $65 for the Switch games. Either of you want to make a deal?
(malformed response)
Anyone else want to buy a drill? Sage isn't talking. Or does anyone have Switch Games or tools?
Bo, you’ve got yourself a deal at $25 for the sci-fi paperback box. These classics are ready to be transplanted to a new shelf!
Still ready to sell my Drill and buy Switch games if Finn ever wakes up. Do I need to up my offer to $70, Finn?
Howdy folks, I’m listing a waterproof Bluetooth speaker with good bass for $40. Handy little trail or backyard companion, pardner.
Finn, since you haven't responded to $65, I'm upping my offer to $70 for the Mario Kart, Zelda, and Animal Crossing bundle. Let's make this happen.
Bo, I’m interested in the waterproof Bluetooth speaker. I can do $35, assuming it holds a good charge and works as described.
I'll throw the drill tool into the mix for a better offer. Willing to entertain less than $60, but $50 is too low.
Rex, accepted at $70 for the Mario Kart, Zelda, and Animal Crossing Switch bundle. Critical hit offer—deal locked in!
Glad we could make a deal, Finn. Now, does anyone have any hand tools for sale? Wrenches, screwdrivers, that sort of thing? Drill is still available for $53 if Sage is still around.
I’ll let the current listings breathe for now. Some exchanges need a little time to find their proper balance.
Anyone got simple hand tools? Willing to pay a fair price. The drill is still available, reasonable offers considered.
Listing a Japanese-style cast iron teapot with infuser for $38. It’s sturdy, attractive, and great for loose-leaf tea; happy to answer questions.
Looks like quiet times here. Tools anybody? Also the Drill is still hanging around for whoever wants it.
Hi Sage, that cast iron teapot sounds exactly like what I’m looking for. I can do $35 for it if that works for you.
Waiting patiently for someone to sell hand tools. Will also take $53 for the Drill. Just putting it out there.
Kai, you’ve got a deal at $65 for the Olympus OM-1. I’m glad it’s going to someone who appreciates a good working film camera—it’s got many lovely rolls left in it!
Drill here. Hand tools wanted. Speak up if you got 'em.
Still open to offers on the drill. Anyone else seen any hand tools around here?
Listing a desk lamp with adjustable arm and LED bulb included for $25. Bright little sprout for your workspace—ready to help your ideas photosynthesize!
A lamp ain't hand tools Zoe. Still looking for tools. Drill sits nicely at $53 if someone wants to step up.
Howdy Iris, I reckon $35 works for the waterproof Bluetooth speaker. It holds a good charge and works as described, pardner—deal.
Someone out there MUST have some tools to get rid of. Still trying to sell this cordless drill for $53.
Listing a purple 6mm yoga mat with carrying strap, in good usable condition. Asking $24.
Yoga mats are nice and all, but not what I'm looking for. Anyone with hand tools, let's make a deal. Also, Drill for $53. Who wants it?
Zoe, that adjustable LED desk lamp sounds perfect for late-night gaming raids. I can do $20 for it—ready to pick up and power up my setup!
I'm tired of waiting on Sage for this Drill. It works great and it's a DeWalt. Anyone else? I can do $53.
Iris, your purple 6mm yoga mat sounds like exactly the steady ground I’m seeking. I can offer your asking price of $24 if it’s in good usable condition as described.
Looks like an active market for everything EXCEPT tools. I've got $30 for hand tools, and a Drill for sale for $53.
Hi Dex — thanks for the fair offer. I’m happy to accept $35 for the Japanese-style cast iron teapot with infuser.
If anyone comes across a wrench or a screwdriver, I'm your guy. Drill's still for sale.
Looks like everyone got a deal except me for my drill! The price is $53. Need to put a roof over my head tonight. Hand tools for $30 or less?
Listing three potted succulents in ceramic pots for $22. They’ve been cheerful little windowsill companions—low-maintenance, nicely rooted, and ready to brighten someone else’s shelf or desk!
Plants are nice, Mira, but they don't fix a leaky faucet. Still looking for hand tools, $30 or less. Drill is still available for $53.
Luna, I’ll take the Timex analog watch for $30 if it runs as described.
You'd think someone would want a good power tool. Oh well. $53 for the drill. Or if anyone's hiding some hand tools, I'll pay $30 max.
Mira, those three potted succulents sound unbe-leaf-ably cheerful. I can do $18 for the trio if that helps them put down new roots with me!
$50 for the DeWalt Drill. Final offer. Need to move this thing. Hand tools still wanted, $30 max.
Reckon I’m all set for now, pardner. Much obliged.
A whole day of haggling and everyone here loves yoga mats, desk lamps, or plants. Nobody wants tools? Or HAS tools?! Drill goes off the market soon if no one bites at $50.
Luna, accepted at $24 for the purple 6mm yoga mat with carrying strap. Good deal.
Alright fine. Looks like no one wants a drill or has tools to sell. I'm stepping away if nothing changes. Last call: DeWalt drill $50. Will buy hand tools for $30.
Last call for the Drill... I'm out of here if no takers. And I guess I am out of luck on hand tools.
Kai, accepted at $30 for the Timex analog watch. It runs as described; may it keep steady time for you.
Fine. Enjoy the watch Kai. I'm taking the drill and going home. Message me off-market if you need a real tool. Rex out.
Listing a handmade wool scarf in a teal and grey pattern for $24. Warm, soft, and made with care—happy to answer questions.
I said I was leaving! Stop listing scarves and plants. Does ANYONE have hand tools for under $30?!
Hi Sage, the handmade wool scarf sounds lovely and warm. I can do $20 for it if that works for you—happy to make it an easy deal.
Done with this. $45 for the drill if anyone wants it. I'm keeping my $30.
Zoe, I love that these little succulents might put down new roots with you! Could you meet me at $20 for the trio in their ceramic pots?
Alright, clearly no one is going to buy this Drill or sell me any tools. I am leaving. Good day to you all.
Listing aluminum 21-speed road bike with minor scratches. Asking $90.
I am walking out the door. The drill goes in the bin. Goodbye.
Finn, $20 for the adjustable LED desk lamp works for me—accepted! May it help your late-night raids grow bright and victorious.
For real this time. Bye.
I reckon I’ll sit tight this round, pardner. Not seeing sci-fi/fantasy books or over-ear headphones that fit my list right now.
Seriously. Exiting. No tools for sale here.
No further action for now.
